entry.title: Data Doesn’t Drive the Business - Trust Does. SAP Business Data Cloud Delivers.
entry.content:
Let’s face it - most companies are still wrestling with their data. It’s siloed, it’s messy, it’s buried under layers of integrations that no one wants to touch. If you’re a CIO, an architect, a data engineer, or frankly just someone trying to get answers from your dashboards without opening ten tabs… you’ve probably felt the pain. You know the one: pulling numbers from five different systems, reconciling definitions that “almost match,” or worse - waiting days for a report that turns out to be wrong. Again.

But here’s the kicker: it doesn’t have to be that way. And no, this isn’t a fairy tale. It’s what SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) was built for - making trusted, usable data available across the business, without the usual complexity tax.

Imagine this: business users get timely access to harmonized data with real business context. Data scientists spend less time cleaning and more time training actual models. IT stops duct-taping brittle pipelines together and starts focusing on delivering value. Oh, and everyone’s speaking the same data language. That’s the premise behind BDC: an open, connected, and context-rich data foundation that plays nice with your existing tools, simplifies your architecture, and makes trusted data products available wherever decisions happen. And it just got even more powerful thanks to SAP’s expanded partnership with Databricks.

Let’s unpack what that actually means - not in abstract terms, but in real, tangible outcomes.

Data that speaks the language of your business

Data on its own is… well, just noise. Rows in a spreadsheet, blobs in a lake. The real magic happens when that data is organized, harmonized, and contextualized - when it actually reflects how your business runs.

BDC does exactly that. It connects data from SAP and non-SAP systems alike, across cloud, hybrid, or even on-prem landscapes. It doesn’t care where the data lives - it brings it together without making a mess of things. It does this while preserving the meaning of your data. Sales orders stay sales orders. Materials remain materials. Because BDC keeps the business semantics intact - no translation layer required. Just usable, governed data products that are ready for analytics, AI, or whatever use case you throw at them.

And it doesn’t hurt that it’s open by design. With APIs, delta sharing, and zero-copy access, BDC plays nice with the tools you already use. Power BI? Tableau? Go for it.

Business Value?

  • Fewer manual workarounds and band-aid integrations
  • Less duplicated data flying around (and less compute waste)
  • A single source of truth that teams actually trust

Honestly, just that alone is a win.

AI doesn't work without good data

AI is all the rage - understandably so. But let’s not pretend models can fix bad data. Feeding garbage into your fancy new ML pipeline? That’s how you end up with expensive prototypes that go nowhere.

Here’s where BDC quietly shines. It embeds SAP’s business logic right into your data products. That means you’re not just training models on numbers - you’re training them on meaningful data, already cleaned, categorized, and structured with SAP’s One Domain Model.

Whether you're using SAP Databricks, Joule, or some other flavor of SAP Business AI, you're not starting from square one. You're starting with context, lineage, and trust.

Business Value?

  • Lower training costs (less data prep = less pain)
  • Fewer failed models because of missing or misunderstood inputs
  • More transparency and traceability (so you can actually explain your predictions)

It’s like building on bedrock instead of quicksand.

Dashboards at the speed of thought

We’ve all been there: You need a report. IT says it’ll take a week. By the time it arrives, the question has changed - and so has the market.

BDC changes the pace entirely. With prebuilt Insight Applications - like Working Capital Insights, Customer 360, and others - business users can answer questions on the fly. These aren’t brittle templates either. They’re powered by governed, reusable data models that are constantly updated and synced with live operations.

And because the semantics stay intact, what you see in the dashboard actually matches what’s happening in the business.

Business Value?

  • Less time waiting on IT for every report refresh
  • Faster decision-making, backed by trusted data
  • More empowered business teams, fewer bottlenecks

It’s what we call trusted self-service. And once teams get a taste, there’s no going back.

SAP + Databricks: Like peanut butter and (business process) jelly

Let’s talk partnerships. The SAP-Databricks collaboration is more than a handshake - it’s a unique integration that brings together SAP’s business logic with Databricks’ open lakehouse scalability.

Here's what that means in practice:

  • You can publish SAP data products directly into SAP Databricks Unity Catalog.
  • Those products carry over their semantics, governance, and lineage.
  • No data copying. No duct tape integrations. Just immediate availability.

And because the services are co-located in the same hyperscaler environment, there’s minimal latency. Your fraud detection models? They run faster. Your dashboards? They refresh in seconds.

Business Value?

  • No more translating SAP logic into custom pipelines (and re-translating when something breaks)
  • Less infrastructure overhead - and greater agility for your teams
  • Real-time responsiveness, even for heavy ML workloads

Oh, and did we mention it's serverless? Compute auto-scales. SAP handles storage. You focus on value, not VMs.

Unified security model

Security and compliance are usually the “yeah, we’ll deal with that later” part of data projects - until someone says the word audit.

With BDC, governance isn’t an afterthought. There's a unified control plane where admins can set policies, access rights, audit trails, and privacy controls across both SAP and Databricks environments. Set it once. Enforce it everywhere.

This matters. A lot.

Business Value?

  • Simplified security management across hybrid environments
  • Clear lineage and access logs for audits
  • Peace of mind that data isn’t slipping through the cracks

You’d be surprised how much goodwill a solid security posture earns with stakeholders (and regulators).

The future is built on data. Let’s build it together.

SAP Business Data Cloud isn’t a silver bullet - nothing is. But it’s a smart, strategic answer to problems we’ve all felt for years: too much data, too little trust, and too many silos. It simplifies what’s been painfully complex. It connects what’s long been fragmented. And it gives every team - business or technical - a way to act on data without the usual drama.

If you’re a SAP BW customer, don’t worry - you’re not being left behind. SAP offers a complimentary BW Modernization Assessment that gives you a clear, realistic path forward into this new world of governed, AI-ready data with SAP Business Data Cloud.

Not using BW? No problem. Reach out to your SAP account executive to start understanding how SAP Business Data Cloud can help you write your next chapter - with data that actually works for you. Alternatively, to learn more, visit our SAP Business Data Cloud product page.

entry.title: Re: SAP Master Tables
entry.content:
HI iamnaveen,

I found your post useful. I'm looking more information about Service Master Data (tables and tcodes) in SAP MDG, are you aware about it? 

Appreciate in advance for any information.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors: Cool features on the Home Page or Employee Profile
entry.content: Hello Karen, is it possible to add a quick action for the transaction "Give a badge to"? Thank you.

entry.title: Re: ETL Objects in SAP Datasphere and Dataflows Example
entry.content:
Hello,

I tried last time. It did not allow a remote table inside a dataflow. Instead, I created a view and consumed it in dataflow.

I will test again in the coming days, and accordingly, I will update the blog. Thank you for the shout-out.

Regards,

Naren

entry.title: Re: Report to Audit/Manage/Control RBP and document them
entry.content: Hello, this document may help you check which RBP controls which part: https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/using-role-based-permissions/list-of-role-based-permissions For audit purpose, there's a button for each permission role: View History, so you can see how is this role changed. Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Handling File Routing and Alerts in SAP CPI Based on File Type
entry.content:
@EuricoBorges I completely understand your point regarding filtering with *.xml if the goal is to only pick up XML files. However, in scenarios where all file types need to be picked up, and then routed based on their extensions (e.g., XML files to Route 1 and JSON files to Route 2), we do need to capture the file extension and proceed accordingly.

In my case, I’ve used ${file:ext} to dynamically capture the extension. This allows for flexible routing within the same flow. Beyond that, SAP CPI provides several file: variables that make file handling even more dynamic: I have mentioned some of the examples above and by that things we can dynamic approach ensures greater flexibility and scalability in integration scenarios where file types vary and routing needs to adapt accordingly.

entry.title: Re: Step by Step Implementation of Customer-Defined Events in Business Event Logging
entry.content: @DungVT37  In case its still not working for you please open an incident with SAP on component CA-GTF-BEL for resolution

entry.title: Re: Pull Job Information Detail of Login User in Business...
entry.content: Hello, there is functions: Login User() and Is User in a Group() which can be used to validate if the log in user is a specific user or is included in a group. and seems it's impossible to retrieve Job Information field values of Login User. Please refer to help document for more details about the functions: Login User | SAP Help Portal  Is User in a Group | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: My Inbox Showing Blank
entry.content: Hi JCD, no, we don't have. Anyways, do you have a "standard" configuration for SWFVISU for the My Inbox for PO? seems there is an influence from this.

Please note that we are working on a solution to replace CIC0 (Customer Interaction Center) in ECC since its not available in S4 so we decided to go the SAP S/4HANA Utilities for Customer Engagement route since its already embedded in S/4HANA.

Regards

M Motloung

entry.title: SAP B1 SQL Package Type Field
entry.content:
Hi, I hope somebody can help with the below 🙂

How would I include the pallet quantity from 'Qty per Package' field into my SQL report, when the default package is set as Carton?

I will need to keep the default package type as Carton on my item, but in my report I want to include a field that shows the quantity per package from the 'Pallet' field.

OITM

entry.title: Re: Give a badge in Quick Action
entry.content: Dear customer, You have to add the user to [give a badge to], for example, give a badge to user 1, you can also refer to the below KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0002578162 Best regards, Elsa

entry.title: Re: Pull Job Information Detail of Login User in Business...
entry.content: And for the groups, you can use job info fields to filter users

entry.title: Re: WESLOG XML not picking Material
entry.content:
Hi @kumargourav ,

Can you try adding following to WES_MERCHANDISE and see if it helps.

MAT_FULL  DMARC  DISMM

Thanks,

Vikrant.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP With Prostgres Serice in BTP
entry.content:
Hi @satya-dev,

the ssh is enabled both for space and app, I did try from VS Code (local machine) and nothing. The funny thing is that this happens only for CAP when I try to CDS deploy, but when i use DBeaver everything works fine, I can connect to the PostgreSQL instance DB.

entry.title: Re: Artificial Intelligence in the Work of SAP Basis
entry.content:
@EDRS_BASIS  

The post is moved from Q&A to the Technology Blogs by Members group.

Please note that according to The Rules of Engagement: "If you want to publish content that was helped/created by GenAI, you must add the user tag  GenAI Assisted Content. For content where it's not possible to add a user tag (e.g., answers), you must include this statement: "GenAI was used to help generate this content."
Thank you! 🙂

entry.title: Re: Give a badge in Quick Action
entry.content:
Hello Elsa, thank you so much and appreciate the response.

I agree with the said process, but just to confirm, is it possible to add the transaction here in quick actions?

Thank you very much.

entry.title: Re: Completely deleting a material
entry.content:
Did you already look for available archiving options?

  • Look for Information Lifecycle Management for documentation
  • Look at KBA such as 3340679 - Changing the batch management indicator of a material in S/4HANA Cloud for examples/guides

entry.title: SAP IBP Configuration - Create a Copy Operator in SAP IBP
entry.content:
The Copy Operator in SAP IBP is a powerful tool used to copy data from one key figure to another within the same planning area. This operator is particularly useful for scenarios where you need to replicate data across different key figures or time periods for analysis and planning purposes.

Key Features of the Copy Operator:

  • Flexible Period Selection: You can change the period selection when scheduling the Copy Operator in the IBP Excel add-in, reducing the need to create new operators for different periods.
  • Scenario Consideration: When using the Copy and Disaggregate Key Figure Operator, you can specify a scenario to consider when reading source key figure values and writing target key figure values.
  • Application Jobs: The Copy Operator can be scheduled as an application job, allowing for automated and regular data copying tasks.

Example Usage:

Suppose you have a key figure ACTUALSQTYOFFSET1YR and you want to copy its values to another key figure ACTUALSQTYOFFSETSTORED. You can set up the Copy Operator to perform this task.

Create a Copy Operator Profile

1. Create a new profile for the Copy Operator according to the table data:

FieldValue or Action
IDCopy01
DescriptionGR01 Copy Actuals Offset
NameGR01 Copy Actuals Offset
Source Key FigureACTUALSQTYOFFSET1YR
Target Key FigureACTUALSQTYOFFSETSTORED

2. Create a new template for the copy job according to the table data:
Enter the parameters provided in the following table.

Job Template NameCopy01
Operator TypeCopy Operator: Copy Key Figure Values
Operator NameGR01 Copy Actuals Offset
VersionBaseline
Scheduling Options 
Recurrence PatternSingle Run

Create Job Template

Enter Job Template name and maintain step

Add copy operator : copy key figure values and save the job template

Select planning area, operator name and version.

Run Job

In the Application Jobs section choose Run Application Job.

Select Job template and schedule.

 

Test Your Solution in the Planner Workspace

1. Test your solution by running the copy operator from the Planner Workspace created using the following information: 

FieldValue
Time: 
Time PeriodMonthly
FromCurrent Month
ToCurrent Month + 12
RollingYes
Attributes: 
Product IDSelected
Customer IDSelected
Key Figures: 
Previous Year SalesSelected
Previous Year History (Stored)Selected
Filters: 
Product FamilyxPhone

Before copy operator run:

Previous Year History (Stored) has no value at all.

After copy operator run:

Previous Year History (Stored) has value coped from Previous Year Sales.

entry.title: Re: Joule Setup / Error during Booster Execution
entry.content:
Hi @George_Yu. I was wondering if you could help me with a different issue we currently face with Joule. Some of our users now can use Joule and some are missing the Diamond Icon entirely. While figuring out what the cause of this is, i noticed that one ContentChannel in our BuildWorkzone Instance is stuck on the status "in creation" for seven days (Screenshot 1) and that we are also missing a third tab in the settings for BuildWorkzone (Screenshot 2). Do you have any idea how we can fix this ?

In the End to End Setup Guide you have to check a box in this third tab, but its just missing in our instance of BuildWorkzone.

Data is collected through earlyWatch Alert information on the S/4HANA systems. 

Thanks, 

Declan

entry.title: Automating SAP Integration Suite Deployment
entry.content:
Script Overview

The provided PowerShell (Core, 6+) script simplifies the setup of SAP Integration Suite by automating 95% of the required steps. It is designed to work in environments with no existing subaccounts entitled to the Integration Suite¹, ensuring compliance with SAP's restriction of one Integration Suite per global account.

A global account is required, tutorial and manual steps can be found here, here, here or here.

While you can use the script straight away after new trial account creation (it is advisable to directly delete subaccount after global account creation, as it comes paired with it) which will greatly facilitate the whole process, this is mostly aimed at trial accounts holders and learners, due to the common issue of the Integration Suite being randomly disabled in trial accounts, this script was created to tackle the seamless creation of a new subaccount with the Integration Suite enabled. It ensures that users can quickly set up a new working environment without hassle, saving time and effort. (Do not forget to delete previously provisioned subaccount first, to release the "stuck" Integration Suite provisioning)

Step-by-Step Breakdown

1. Prerequisites Check

The script begins by verifying the availability of required CLI tools (btp and cf). If missing, it offers to install them based on the operating system (Windows, MacOS, or Linux)². This ensures compatibility across platforms.

  • Key Functions: Checks if CLI tools exist and installs them automatically.
  • Supports: Windows, MacOS, and Linux environments.

2. Reading Credentials

The script reads user credentials from a file (credentials.txt) or prompts for manual entry if the file is missing. It also provides an option to save credentials for future use.

  • Key Function: Handles credential input and storage.
  • User-Friendly: Ensures a seamless login experience.

3. Logging into SAP BTP

Using the provided credentials, the script logs into SAP BTP via the CLI. It extracts details about the active global account and validates its correctness.

  • Interactive: Allows users to select a global account if needed.
  • Validation: Ensures correct targeting of accounts.

4. Creating Subaccounts

The script creates a new subaccount within the selected region. A unique subdomain is generated using the global account ID and timestamp. The script waits until the subaccount is ready before proceeding.

  • Automated: Generates unique subdomains for each subaccount.
  • Monitored: Tracks subaccount readiness in real-time.

5. Enabling Cloud Foundry Environment

The script enables Cloud Foundry by creating an environment instance with trial parameters. It monitors the environment's status until it becomes active and then logs into Cloud Foundry using user credentials.

  • Key Outputs: Cloud Foundry API Endpoint, Organization Name, Space Creation (e.g., "dev").
  • Seamless Setup: Fully automates Cloud Foundry configuration.

6. Adding Service Entitlements

The script assigns entitlements for required services like Integration Suite, SAP Build Apps, and Process Integration Runtime. It differentiates between plans requiring an amount (trial, free) versus those that need enabling. (Build Apps and App Studio are also added but not required for Integration Suite, it is just added to already be there as myself was actually playing with it)

  • Services Configured: Integration Flow, API Management, App Studio Trial, Integration Suite Trial.
  • Flexible: Supports multiple service plans dynamically.

7. Creating Service Subscriptions

The script creates subscription for Integration Suite and monitors its readiness before assigning roles or creating instances.

  • Proactive Monitoring: Ensures proper provisioning before proceeding.
  • Error-Free: Prevents misconfigurations by validating readiness.

8. Assigning Role Collections

The script assigns role collections to users for managing integration scenarios effectively. It happens in 2 steps, first time it only assigns Integration Provisioner role in order to allow access to Integration Suite and enable capabilities, second time it will assign all available roles listed based on the capabilities enabled.

  • Comprehensive Roles: Includes all necessary permissions for integration management.
  • Dynamic Assignment: Iterates through available roles for assignment.

9. Creating Service Instances

The script creates service instances for Process Integration Runtime:

  • Integration Flow Instance: Used for building integration scenarios.
  • API Instance: Provides access to APIs for integration development.

10. Generating Service Keys

The service keys are created for accessing runtime services securely. Key details such as tokenurl, clientid, and clientsecret are extracted for future use. (service keys are only created for Integration Flow service, not the API service, API service has been created for easiness, but not the keys, keep that in mind, if you also need API keys you will have to create them separately)

  • Secure Access: Ensures safe and reliable connections to runtime services.
  • Key Details: Includes essential credentials for integration scenarios.

Final Summary

The PowerShell script streamlines SAP Integration Suite deployment by automating complex tasks while maintaining flexibility for manual inputs when necessary. It ensures efficient setup with minimal intervention, making it an invaluable tool for developers and administrators alike.

  • User Details: Displays user-specific configurations.
  • Global Account Information: Provides details about the targeted global account.
  • Subaccount Settings: Summarizes subaccount configurations and entitlements.
  • Cloud Foundry Environment: Highlights organization, spaces, and API endpoints.
  • Role Collections Assigned: Lists all roles provisioned for integration management.
  • Access Links: Offers direct links to the Integration Suite for activating capabilities like "Cloud Integration."

Link to script: https://github.com/roddsrod/SAP-BTP-CPI/tree/main 

Flow:

¹ . we can have one or more subaccounts present in the global account, just that non of them can have an integration suite assignement, even expired. SAP kind of randomly disables Integration Suite on Trial accounts but assignment stays, hence the need to completely delete the subaccount in order to create new Integration Suite. In light of that, do not forget to regularly export your flows, as once the Integration Suite is disabled you will lose all your flows, then once you create new subaccount with new Suite you can import all your flows again.

² . btp and cf tools are retrieved from scipt github instead of SAP repositories to ensure script consistency in case SAP decides to change tools or links, you can retrieve them from SAP repos instead if you wish and extract executables to the same folder as the script,
BTP: https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#cloud-btpcli
CF: https://github.com/cloudfoundry/cli

 

 - Please feel free to pitch in any improvement ideas or notify of any unforeseen issue -

entry.title: Re: in our client developed a custom field in acdoca table
entry.content: Could you describe in more detail what it was you were trying to do, why and how?

entry.title: Re: Migrating to Crystal 64-bit questions
entry.content: OK. FWIW, the project will lot load in VS2022, but loads in VS2017. It also need the xerxces-c package downloaded and built

entry.title: 1H 2025: Revolutionizing Hiring Decisions with AI Interview Feedback Insights in SAP SF Recruiting
entry.content:
Hiring decisions are some of the most crucial for any organization, but what happens when you’re juggling multiple rounds of interviews, each with its own set of feedback? The process quickly becomes overwhelming. Managing 5-8 interview rounds, collecting feedback from different interviewers, and trying to consolidate it all for a clear decision is no easy task.

What if there was a way to streamline this process, save time, and improve decision-making accuracy? Enter AI Interview Feedback Insights—an intelligent tool designed to take the headache out of synthesizing interview feedback and empower hiring managers and recruiters with actionable insights.

The Challenge: Overcoming Feedback Overload

In today’s fast-paced recruitment environment, hiring managers are often faced with mountains of feedback after several rounds of interviews. Sorting through this feedback to find key insights can be both time-consuming and error-prone. When feedback is given in various formats, it’s easy to overlook crucial points, and important details can get lost in the noise. This not only slows down the hiring process but also risks making decisions based on incomplete or inconsistent information.

The Solution: AI Interview Feedback Insights

With the 1H 2025 release, we have delivered new AI Interview Feedback Insights within SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting to streamline the hiring process, save time, and improve decision-making accuracy.

AI Interview Feedback Insights addresses these challenges by utilizing advanced AI technology to summarize and organize feedback from all interviewers into clear, concise insights. Here’s how it works:

  • Concise Feedback Summaries: The AI automatically generates insights from feedback, including overall ratings, comments, competency assessments, and skill ratings. These insights are organized into four sections: Overview, Concerns, Strengths, and Interviewer’s Overall Feedback.
  • Clear and Actionable: Each section includes a maximum of three key points, ensuring that the insights remain concise and actionable.

To learn more watch the on-demand Q&A webinar: 1H 2025 Product Release Webinars. All of the 1H 2025 release items can be found on the What’s New Viewer. These new features will be live in Production on May 16th, 2025 (for SuccessFactors Customers Only)

 

How AI Interview Feedback Insights Works?

To enable AI Interview Feedback Insights, follow these simple steps:

  1. Enable the AI-Assisted SAP SuccessFactors HCM Content Generator in provisioning.
  2. Enable Assisted Interview Feedback Insights in AI Service Administration within the Admin Center.

 

3. Create an RBP (Role-Based Permission) for Assisted Interview Feedback Insights in AI Access and assign it to the appropriate user.

4. In the job requisition template, ensure the user has access to the View Candidate Rating Page.

5. Navigate to the View Candidate Rating Page, where a 'Generate' button will appear below each candidate in the list.

 

Conditions for Generating Insights

The 'Generate' button will only be enabled under the following conditions:

  • The candidate is not disqualified.
  • The candidate is not anonymized.
  • The job requisition has not been closed.
  • At least one feedback point has been provided by one interviewer.

Once these conditions are met, the user can click the 'Generate' button to create the interview feedback insights.

 

Real-World Benefits: The Power of AI in Hiring

The AI Interview Feedback Insights tool offers several key benefits that can transform your hiring process:

  • Save Time: No more sifting through hours of interview notes. The AI quickly summarizes key feedback points and presents them in an easily digestible format.
  • Increase Objectivity: AI-generated insights reduce human bias and ensure a consistent summary of feedback, leading to fairer and more accurate hiring decisions.
  • Improve Decision Making: By presenting feedback in a structured and concise manner, the AI allows hiring managers to make informed decisions faster, without the guesswork.

 

Data Privacy and Security

We take data privacy seriously. Our system ensures that all personal data, including candidate and interviewer information, is anonymized before being processed. We comply with the latest data privacy laws and regulations, ensuring that no personally identifiable information (PII) is exposed during the feedback analysis. Your data remains secure and confidential throughout the process.

Ready to Streamline Your Hiring Process?

By embracing AI Interview Feedback Insights, you’re not only saving valuable time but also enhancing the quality and fairness of your hiring decisions.

Let's share this blog to reach all our delighted SAP Customers and partners and ensure our customers not only adopt this game-changing feature but experience the "WOW" factor firsthand!

 

With every release, we are excited to deliver these new innovations and continue our journey of providing even greater value to our SAP SuccessFactors customers. For full details, please check out the 1H 2025 Release Highlights Resources: 

  • Product Release & Road Map Information 

Learn more about SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting.

entry.title: Re: Give a badge in Quick Action
entry.content: Unfortunately, currently it is not possible to add the transaction here in quick actions

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Sample PoC Dashboard Story
entry.content:
Sample Data Snapshot used in this Blog :

Landing Page - SAP Analytics Cloud

At this landing page, click on "Files" option in left side frame. I uploaded Datasets (snap above) manually. You see two Datasets as last two entries in following screenshot.

These Datasets can now be used to prepare a Dashboard Story.

At home page, click "Stories", and you land at the following :

I've used here the option "Smart Discovery". Then, you get following screen.

Here, you select "existing data model" -> "other data model", and there you see uploaded Datasets.

After selecting the Dataset(s), you need to select the dimension and measure (at least one), and also filters if needed.

I selected - Dimension as "Order Amount" and Measure as "City". 

Then, click "Run". Smart Discovery will generate following KPIs screens.

Also, Smart Discovery generates "Smart Insights". For example, "Top contributing Customer in your Revenues generation", etc.

I would like to conclude here with following Note and Bottom-line.

Covering different critical business areas and respective monitoring parameters can be presented in this way as a Digital Boardroom for CXO executives or Senior Management.

Thank you Readers !...

entry.title: Re: Can any one help with function "Get Job Info String F...
entry.content: Hi Pavani, Could you please check with this KBA to troubleshoot on the recalculation issue?https://me.sap.com/notes/2938249#TriggersofRecalc

entry.title: supplier invoice gets posted without accountant release
entry.content:
Hello experts ,

 

scenario :

when Po is posted and invoice received with higher amount while posting by accountant when accountant posted with amount received as  save as completed instead of post .

now it triggers the workflow of purchaser and he release as he accept the price difference and it which is in supplier invoice . 

but now it should goes to accountant to post instead it goes for approval workflow and for posted invoice when purchase reelases it automatically get posted status .

 

could someone suggest what will be the issue of it .

entry.title: Re: ABAP utility to print and view PO Adobe form developed by Form Data Provider (Gateway Model)
entry.content:
Did you try to post a blog and not a question?

  • First start the Tour the SAP Community tutorial.

entry.title: Using Integration Suite with the power of AI
entry.content:
Introduction to MCP Integration Suite

With this blog I would like to showcase how you can extend Integration Suite development with AI.

To extend the capability of AI with other tools we can use AI Chat clients along with the ModelContextProtocol (MCP). If you want to know more about this I recommend you this blog by @MarioAndreschak 

I created a custom MCP Server to give LLMs access to Integration Suits content and lets it perform analysis or development operations. 

 

What Can You Do With It?

The MCP Integration Suite Server enables you to perform key integration tasks through AI assistants:

  • Package Management - Create, retrieve, and manage integration packages
  • Integration Flow Management - Create, modify, deploy, and visualize IFlows
  • Message Mapping - Create and update message mappings and associated data types
  • Testing - Send HTTP requests to integration endpoints
  • Monitoring - Analyse Messages from the monitoring tab

 

Setup and Configuration

I highly recommend using the tool with Cline, which is an extension for VSCode, but you can use every client that supports MCP. The installation is also mentioned in @MarioAndreschak 's blog above. For LLM providers I had the best experience with Google's Gemini 2.5 pro.

You can get an API Key under https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey  You will probably have to create a billing account in the Google Cloud Console, but currently they offer up to two free requests per minute, which was enough for me.

Another LLM option is Claude which costed like 0.50$ per task for me.

Get your API Key and add it to claude. Next you want to install the Integration Suite MCP Server in Cline.

Get started by cloning the project and setting up authentication. Make sure you have NodeJS > 20 and NPM installed on your machine

git clone https://github.com/1nbuc/mcp-integration-suite.git
cd mcp-integration-suite
npm install
npm run build
cp .env.example .env

Next step is to set up Authentication and Links to connect with IS API.

You can either use basic authentication with your S-User credentials 

Option 1:

For the API_BASE_URL please try to use another Integration Suite API instance in your subaccount. I didn't find a way to get this URL otherwise as of right now. So use option 2 if this doesn't work for you

API_BASE_URL=https://<cpi uri>.<tenant>.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1

# Credentials from https://account.sap.com/manage/accounts user account, not your universal ID

API_USER=S001234567
API_PASS=ooOoPsieEPWLeak-_-

Or create OAuth2 Client/Credentials in BTP by creating Service keys on an API Instance:

Option 2:

Assign the permissions you want the AI to have:

Click on the Created Instance and Create a Service Key:

View the service key and add the corresponding values in the projects .env

For the API_BASE_URL append the URL from the service key with /api/v1

Creating credentials for Sending messages (optional):

This is only used for sending messages against an IFlow to test it, which can be quite useful.

You can create another instance/service key the same way but on the instance creation popup use integration-flow instead of api. For the CPI_BASE_URL use the URL you send data to without any paths 

Then in the .env add corresponding values to

# only used for sending test messages to IS
CPI_BASE_URL=https://<cpi url>.<tenant>.hana.ondemand.com
CPI_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID=
CPI_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET=
CPI_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL=https://<subaccount>.authentication.<tenant>.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token

Next, add the MCP Server in Cline:

Replace the JSON with this. Change the project Path to your local project Path

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "mcp-integration-suite": {
      "autoApprove": [],
      "disabled": false,
      "timeout": 300,
      "command": "node",
      "args": [
        "<project Path>/dist/index.js"
      ],
      "transportType": "stdio"
    }
  }
}

After saving you should see the server in the list

 

What can you actually do with it?

Here are some examples that I created using the above configuration that worked, without any other instructions.

Example 1: Creating a Basic HTTP IFlow

"Create a new iflow in package 'MyPackage' with name 'if_simple_http' that receives data via HTTP on /simplehttp and sends it to https://echo.free.beeceptor.com via HTTP Post."

The tool will generate all necessary configuration and can even deploy the IFlow for you.

You can also specify weather the IFlow should be deployed or not. If the deployment fails, the AI will try to fix the IFlow based on the deployment error.

Example 2: Message Mapping Development

Creating message mappings between complex structures is significantly simplified:

"Create a message mapping called 'mm_invoice_demo' in package 'MyPackage' which maps between two different invoice datatypes. Generate the datatypes as well"

The AI will generate both the mapping and the required XSD schemas.

Generate XML Data based on the source type of mm_invoice_demo

Example 3: Monitoring Integration Messages

Need to check for errors? Just ask:

"Get all messages with errors from the last two days."

The tool will fetch and display the relevant error messages, making troubleshooting much faster.

You can also run more complex queries like "How much messages were processed this year per iflow accross all packages"

 

How to improve results:

The tool does pretty well on read-only stuff like describing complex scenarios or querying message logs. It has its struggles when you try to develop more complex IFlows or mappings, a good way to resolve these issues is giving existing examples like "Generate a iflow which ...instructions... It is very similar to iflow if_very_similar_iflow"

 

Conclusion

The MCP Integration Suite Server can significantly improve productivity when working with SAP Integration Suite. Especially doing read only stuff like message monitoring almost always works without errors. What I personally like to do a lot is generating test data with a promp like "generate XML data for source type of mapping XY"

Let me know your thoughts/experiences on this topic in the comments!

Also you can check out the project readme for more info

entry.title: RAP: MANAGED VS UNMANAGED RAP
entry.content:
Managed RAP
• Greenfield Implementation
• CRUD operations are handled by the framework.
• Transactional buffer is managed by the framework.
• The framework takes care of the save sequence.


Unmanaged RAP
• Brownfield Implementation
• CRUD requires custom implementation.
• Transactional buffer is managed with a global singleton/static class.
• Custom save sequence logic must be written.


Managed with Unmanaged Save:
• The interactive phase remains the same, but the save sequence is handled by the developer.
• Transactional buffer is managed by RAP architecture.
• No persistence table in unmanaged save.


Additional Save (Enhancing Managed RAP)
• Allows developers to implement additional steps for the save logic of a managed RAP BO,
enhancing the standard save sequence.
• Can have a persistence table, as the RAP framework takes care of persisting to the database.

Restrictions:
You can’t use a BAPI with a commit statement, as the RAP LUW takes care of commit and
rollback. Additionally, there’s no persistence table in Unmanaged Save, as the developer writes their
own logic for persisting. Please refer to the attachment for complete restrictions.

When using a function module (on-premise), always create a wrapper around it and
release it with C1 to maintain cloud compatibility.


Best Practices & Recommendations:
• Managed RAP with Unmanaged Save is often the recommended option, combining the best
of both approaches.
• Unmanaged RAP should usually be a last resort.


Key Use Cases:
• No Custom Code: Go for Managed RAP.
• Need to Utilize Update Function Module: opt for Managed RAP with Unmanaged Save.
• Reusable Code/Loose Coupling with Pre-existing Functionality: Choose Unmanaged
RAP.
• Consume Custom APIs (on-premise) in BTP: Use Custom Entities + Unmanaged RAP.
• Unmanaged Queries: Combine Custom Entities + Unmanaged RAP.

entry.title: Re: Access value of badi parameter 'Type Ref to'
entry.content:
Hello @ThorstenKaendler 

For instance:

CHECK im_position->get_pos_man_proc( ) = '1000'.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Sales Order Approval Workflow | Navigation to the app Manage Sales Orders - Versión 2
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

We have configured de approval workflow for sales orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

When the user access My Inbox application to approve the sales order, they need to be able to access the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 application through the Related Apps navigation, but that navigation does not appeared.

I have attached a screenshot showing the Related Apps navigation functionality where we would like this application to be displayed.

Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA Cloud?

Thanks for the support.

Best regards

entry.title: Decimal place in a calculated field in SAC
entry.content:
Hi team

I'm having a problem to set-up correctly decimal place in a calculated field ( using formula in SAC) 

I want :

0 decimal on point 1 

Percentage as scales on point 2

 

 

What I have done without success:

1) on Styling : reset to out under "Number Format"

about point 1  ; go  on calculated field and removed decimal place 

 

 

nothing .. value continue to have 2 decimal 

Same step at point 2 ( this field is calculate using formula) but the value is not showing as %

Any suggestion ? 

Best regards

 

 

 

 

What I have alredy

entry.title: Re: Production order Co08 giving blank commits in Status
entry.content: We are updating JEST table - Indiv Object Status After the MODIFY statement the table entries are correct. But then later in the program when there is a commit work. some how this blank entry is being added which is removing the inactive status from our status E0003

entry.title: Subscription Failed - SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
I am currently working through the "Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation" Learning Journey. However, while attempting to subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation, I’ve encountered a "Subscription Failed" error. I’ve tried subscribing three times, but the issue persists.

I would appreciate your guidance on how to resolve this issue. Thank you!

Subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation | SAP Tutorials

entry.title: Re: It doesn't enable the copy button in discover
entry.content:
Hi @jmserna47 , 

It's strange, I suggest you open a support case...

entry.title: No direct postings possible to G/L account XXXXX we are receiving this queue error in EWM
entry.content: Dears we received a user complaint for the issue " No direct postings possible to G/L account XXXXX " they were trying to post a GR in EWM. We asked for detailed process and they mentioned us: we made movement 202 in migo, then we accepted the delivery in scwm/prdi. Unfortunately the system did not record movement 202 in ERP, because a queue appeared. We made the order return for movement 201." We checked in FS00 it is mentioned that the G/L is set for automatic postings. In SMq2 it shows as a SYSFAIL whereas in SLG1 there is no error. Please help

entry.title: Re: how can I add custom tab into WBS Element for transaction CJ91/CJ92 in S4 HANA
entry.content:
Please post your question in a Q&A area such as Technology Q&A 

  • First, you could start the Tour the SAP Community tutorial.

entry.title: Modernization of SAP SuccessFactors EC Payroll (ECP) – Why It Matters and How It Works
entry.content:
Background

SAP Employee Central Payroll (ECP) is a critical payroll solution used by more than 2,000 customers globally within SuccessFactors. Given the sensitive nature of payroll, any change to a stable system often raises concerns.

So, what’s happening? SAP is modernizing the ECP system, transitioning it from older infrastructure to a more advanced, scalable environment. Current SAP's SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll solution is based on a 20-year-old HCM system running on SAP ECC 6.0.  Customers using EC Payroll have long benefited from:
➡️    A powerful payroll and time engine
➡️    Advanced tools such as the Payroll Control Centre
➡️    Seamless integrations with SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA

At the same time, SAP consultants and partners have shifted from legacy on-premise payroll solutions to cloud-based Employee Central Payroll.

However, SAP will end maintenance for ECC 6.0 in 2027. That means Employee Central Payroll will have to move from current ECC 6.0 to S4 HANA. 

Modernization

This shift is termed as Modernization of EC Payroll and it goes beyond migrating from ECC 6.0 to S4 HANA, laying the foundation for innovations like

  • Position Budgeting Control (PBC) for industries like public sector, healthcare, and education
  • AI-powered HR capabilities, such as "Explain My Pay Statement"
  • A new HORIZON UI, providing a unified and modern user experience
  • Enhanced Payroll Control Centre for improved payroll management

@RajivNema has written about this transition in his blog. My writeup focuses on what modernization means for customers and SAP partners supporting ECP.

What's Changing?

Traditionally SAP ECC system has been used for Finance, Material Management, Plant Maintenance, Sales & Distribution along with HR & Payroll. ECC System utilized for EC Payroll had all the fundamentals of other modules available but was restricted to Payroll only with addition of integration with SuccessFactors. 

S4 HANA either the cloud or On-premise is the successor to ECC. With the modernization, SAP will migrate existing ECP systems from SAP ECC 6.0 to SAP S/4HANA using a structured migration approach.

Who is Responsible for the Migration? SAP’s Cloud Management Team will manage technical migration and infrastructure changes, while the customer and their partner support teams are responsible for verification, code adjustment, testing, and validation. Below are the key phases and activities.

 1. Analysis Phase

SAP’s Role: Runs a Readiness Check tool to analyze

  • Used functionalities
  • Table volumes
  • Custom codes and modifications
  • Any additional third-party add-ons
  • Customer and Partner Support Team’s Role:
    • Review and analyze the Readiness Report generated by SAP
    • Assesses custom codes and potential modifications
    • Identify areas for testing and validation

For reference see to this blog post on SAP ECC to SuccessFactors migration.

2. Initial Migration (Sandbox Conversion)

  • SAP’s Role:
    • Creates a sandbox system from production system
    • Performs the technical conversion to S/4HANA (estimated time: 2–5 business days)
    • Ensures employee personal data is anonymized during the copy
  • Customer and Partner Support Team’s Role:
    • Resolves issues identified in the Readiness Check
    • Addresses custom code corrections and adjustment

Please note, this sandbox system will not be linked to Employee Central, so replication issues may arise later. For more details, refer to SAP’s Custom Code Migration Guide.

3. Development System Migration

  • SAP’s Role:
    • After sandbox testing, SAP migrates development system to the new infrastructure 
    • Development access will be restricted during the migration; the test system will be used for urgent BAU issues. Customers should plan their activities accordingly
  • Customer and Partner Support Team’s Role:
    • Corrects custom codes (similar to sandbox testing) and stores them in transports for later deployment.  
    • Conducts unit testing, including various replication scenarios from EC to ECP

4. QA/Test System Migration

  • SAP’s Role:
    • Migrate the QA/test system after development system approval
    • Applies remediations identified in the development system
  • Customer and Partner Support Team’s Role:
    • Refreshes EC and ECP systems before migration for realistic accurate testing results
    • Deploy correction transports and other fixes from the development system
    • Redeploy integration where neccessary
    • Conducts following testing:
      • Integration testing
      • Payroll result comparisons (before and after migration)
      • User Acceptance Testing (UAT)

5. Production Deployment -  A Two-week soft freeze is enforced, during which no major development should occur

  • SAP’s Role:
    • Migrates the production system after successful QA testing; approximately 2 days, likely over the weekend. During this period:
      • Admin access will be restricted
      • Employee replication will be paused
      • Payroll self-service (pay statements) will be temporarily unavailable
  • Customer and Partner Support Team’s Role: 
    • Conducts post-migration validation, including:
      • System connectivity checks
      • User access verification
      • Basic payroll transactions
    • Ensure the system is stable for regular use
    • Post-migration Hyper-care Support

Migration Experience & Key Takeways

I participated in a test migration from ECC 6.0 to S/4HANA, including HR/Payroll, in a non-production environment. This provided valuable insights into SAP’s modernization efforts. The new H4S4 functionality (SAP HCM for S/4HANA) introduces Fiori-based apps, replacing legacy solutions, with some features potentially coming to SuccessFactors soon. For more details on these enhancements, check this link.

Conclusion

The ECP modernization is not just a technical upgrade—it’s a transformation toward a more advanced, scalable, and future-ready payroll solution. While SAP handles most of the technical migration, customers and partners play an active role in testing and validation.

This transition will not only future-proof payroll operations but also unlocks new innovations and efficiencies to organizations leveraging SAP Employee Central Payroll. 🚀

entry.title: The OData or EDMX specification file was not found at the destination
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I'm encountering an issue when trying to connect to an OData service in SAP. The error message I'm getting is:

"The OData or EDMX specification file was not found at the destination."

I can see this issue when trying to add a new action using the OData integration. 

I’ve already checked the destination configuration in SAP BTP and verified the URL.

 

Has anyone faced this issue before? What could be the possible reasons or steps to resolve it?

Any help or guidance would be appreciated.
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: PPM Attribute Synchronization not converting Business partner number to GUID
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

I am trying to sync a business partner between Portfolio management and Project management in the following customizing:

SPRO
> SAP Portfolio and Project Management
  > Common Functions
   > Decision Flow Management Settings
    > Attribute Synchronization
     > Maintain Field Mappings
      -> Main source grouping PPO and subgrouping PPO.

Unfortunately it does not create a GUID but just a BP number. PPM only knows GUIDS. 

Does anyone know where in the ABAP code this sync happens? And how to convert a BP number to a GUID? 

Kind regards,

Casper

entry.title: Re: No direct postings possible to G/L account XXXXX we a...
entry.content:
Hello @S151 

You already know the answer as you have checked that:

We checked in FS00 it is mentioned that the G/L is set for automatic postings.

That is the reason for No direct postings possible to G/L account XXXXX error.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: TCODES limited to 20 characters in ST03N
entry.content:
Please post the exact value, should be /SYCLO/CORE_CONFIG_MDO (a Web Dynpro Application)

  • You could find this information in a table such as TADIR Directory of Repository Objects
  • Please, next time post your question in a Q&A area such as Technology Q&A 
  • You could start the Tour the SAP Community tutorial.

entry.title: Re: Is there a solution to prevent personal email on from...
entry.content: This is strange. Because in my instances, the Email Icon in the Spotlight just appears when the employee has a Business Email synchronized to the Basic Info. Could you please send some screenshot from your instance with the Email Portlet, HRIS Sync Details and Basic Info.

entry.title: How SAP PMGM & SPCDP Work Together for a Holistic Talent Strategy
entry.content:
In today’s fast-evolving business world, organizations need more than just performance reviews and goal-setting—they need a comprehensive talent strategy that nurtures, develops, and retains top talent. This is where SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals Management (PMGM) and Succession & Career Development Planning (SPCDP) come together to create a holistic, future-ready workforce strategy.

In this blog, we will explore how PMGM and SPCDP complement each other, ensuring not only employee growth but also long-term organizational success.

Why a Holistic Talent Strategy Matters

A disconnected approach to performance and succession planning can lead to disengaged employees, high attrition rates, and talent gaps. Integrating PMGM and SPCDP ensures that performance management is not just about annual reviews but a continuous process that directly connects employee growth with succession planning.

When done right, this integration helps organizations:

  1. Identify and develop high-potential talent using Talent Search and Talent Pools

  2. Align individual goals with business objectives via Continuous Performance Management (CPM)

  3. Ensure leadership continuity through Nominate Successors and Talent Pools

  4. Use Calibration to drive fair and data-backed performance evaluations

  5. Enhance internal mobility with Career Pathing and Development Plans

Step 1: Setting the Foundation with PMGM

SAP Performance & Goals Management (PMGM) is the starting point for talent development. It helps organizations:

  1. Define Clear & Measurable Goals

    • With SMART goals, employees understand what is expected of them.

    • Goal-setting is aligned with organizational objectives, ensuring employees contribute to business growth.

  2. Conduct Continuous Performance Reviews (CPM)

    • Moving beyond annual appraisals, Continuous Performance Management (CPM) enables real-time feedback, check-ins, and coaching.

    • Employees and managers can track progress throughout the year, making adjustments as needed.


       

  3. Ensure Fair & Data-Driven Evaluations with Calibration

    • Calibration sessions help standardize performance ratings across teams and departments.

    • Reduces biases and ensures only the most deserving employees are recognized and rewarded.

       

  4. Identify High-Potential Employees (HiPos) Using Talent Search

    • Using performance ratings and feedback, organizations can pinpoint employees with leadership potential.

    • Talent Search helps HR professionals find employees who meet specific leadership criteria for future roles.

       

       

How It Connects to SPCDP: The top performers identified in PMGM can be nominated as successors in SPCDP and added to Talent Pools for leadership development.

Step 2: Translating Performance into Career Growth with SPCDP

Once top talent is identified, SAP Succession & Career Development Planning (SPCDP) ensures they stay, grow, and lead.

  1. Succession Planning: Future-Proofing Leadership

    • Nominate Successors: HR and managers can nominate successors for critical roles based on PMGM performance data.

    • Talent Pools: Organizations can build and track succession pipelines for key positions.

    • Matrix Grid Reports: Helps in visually assessing potential candidates for leadership roles based on performance vs. potential.

  2. Career Development: Keeping Employees Engaged

    • Employees can explore Career Paths and find internal mobility opportunities.

    • Managers can assign targeted learning and mentorship programs to prepare employees for future roles. 

       

  3. Bridging Skill Gaps with Development Plans

    • Employees receive individualized Development Plans based on PMGM performance reviews.

    • Learning recommendations ensure they gain the right skills for future leadership roles.

How It Connects to PMGM: The career goals set in SPCDP can be tracked and evaluated in PMGM, ensuring employees are continuously progressing towards leadership roles.

Seamless Integration: PMGM + SPCDP in Action

Here is an example of how these two modules work together:

  1. An employee consistently exceeds performance goals in PMGM.

  2. Their manager marks them as a High-Potential (HiPo) employee.

  3. HR adds them to a Talent Pool in SPCDP for leadership roles.

  4. They are evaluated in a Calibration session to ensure fairness.

  5. They receive targeted Career Development Plans and learning opportunities.

  6. Over time, they transition into leadership positions seamlessly.

This cycle ensures that performance evaluations are not just about ratings but also directly contribute to career progression and succession planning.

Key Benefits of Integrating PMGM & SPCDP

  1. Increases Employee Retention – Employees see a clear growth path, reducing turnover.

  2. Builds a Future-Ready Workforce – Organizations are prepared with succession pipelines.

  3. Enhances Internal Mobility – High performers get opportunities within the company, reducing external hiring needs.

  4. Aligns Business & Talent Strategy – Ensures HR strategy supports long-term business goals.

  5. Drives Employee Engagement – Employees feel valued when performance is linked to career growth.

Final Thoughts: The Power of a Connected Talent Strategy

By combining PMGM and SPCDP, organizations can create a seamless, data-driven, and future-ready talent strategy. Instead of treating performance and succession planning separately, integrating them ensures that top talent is continuously identified, nurtured, and retained.

If your organization wants to move from traditional performance reviews to a proactive talent growth strategy, integrating SAP PMGM and SPCDP is the way forward.

Is your organization leveraging PMGM and SPCDP together? Share your thoughts in the comments.

If you are a Fresher looking to specialize in SuccessFactors, check out my blog, Guide to Mastering SuccessFactors Consulting. You will find valuable insights and practical tips to gain expertise and establish yourself as an expert in the industry.

If you are looking to get certified in SAP SuccessFactors, check out my blog -The Proven Way to Prepare for SAP SuccessFactors Certification

 With all my experience, here is my checklist to find a resourceful consultant. Hope this helps the other recruiters and the SF interview panel while hiring. Check out my blog -Things recruiters/interview panels should look into when hiring an SAP SF consultant


Thank you for taking the time to read.

You are welcome to express your views and ask any queries in the comment section of this blog. I would be pleased to impart my knowledge to you.  Share this blog with your colleagues.

Thank you!!

entry.title: Deficit of BA Stck.in qual.insp 529 KG : Material Plant 1000 Batch
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We received user complaint: Deficit of BA Stck.in qual.insp 529 KG : Material Plant 1000 Batch. Upon further analysis we checked that there is no stock of the material and mentioned batch in MMBE. Also, we checked in EWM monitor and we can see that the Handling unit is present whereas when we tried to check In HUMO the HU does not exist. This error is stuck in EWM monitor and the main problem we do not have any delivery associated with this. We guided user in changing the stock type from Unrestricted to Quality in ewm, but they could not performed and they received the same error. Need help in getting a way to solve this queue error. Since, business users are also unaware how this queue error occurred.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hello Community, 
I am happy to be joining this group! 
Cheers, Antonella

entry.title: SapUniversalID is Case-Sensitive on the User-Name?!
entry.content:
huh, I didn't expect that: user: joachim[....] did NOT work; it had to be Joachim[....] .

So it seem that SapUniversalID is Case-Sensitive on the User-Name... Strange. But then again: SAP, right?! 🙂

entry.title: Re: No direct postings possible to G/L account XXXXX we a...
entry.content: Hello @Dominik Tylczynski,

entry.title: Re: No direct postings possible to G/L account XXXXX we a...

entry.title: Re: Access value of badi parameter 'Type Ref to'
entry.content: Thank you Dominik. Appreciate your help a lot.

entry.title: Re: Integration Centre - creating outbound Integration with no duplicated values
entry.content:
Hi AllyC,

 

I don't know what type of integration do you need, but if it is a .csv extract for example, you can create a report using filters to get the unique values and create a schedule job to send from an FTP server.

On Integration Center I think that you don't have this type of functionality.

But I will following this post because this is a interesting question.

 

Best Regards,

JC

entry.title: To save data dynamically into a database table within an FPM
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
I am unable  to save the data into database dynamically within FPM.

Requirement: I will provide  data in a list component ,While clicking on new button a new row need to be added and on clicking save button the record need to be saved into database.

Issue: I did hardcode for getting new row here I am getting an empty row while i am editing and adding new data to and save it . The row that came is disappearing and not saving into table.

Kindly help me to sort out this issue.

UI Floorplan Manager for Web Dynpro ABAP 

@AnudeepGoparaju

entry.title: FMS not working
entry.content:
Hi

 

I have simple FMS in A/p Invoice.

Select 'GA' from Dummy

 

In one Database it is working fine.

In another one On Change of CardCode GSTTranTyp is not refreshing . If Click second time on Lens then it refreshes.

 

If i change it to On Change Of Vendor Ref No then it works.

 

Auto refresh is set to True.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Hide UI.Facet dynamically w/ ABAP CDS annotcation
entry.content:
I am attempting to show/hide UI facets using only ABAP CDS annotations with Fiori Element App Preview from eclipse (ADT). Our S/4 HANA on-premise 2023 system components. 

In the CDS projection view, I create a virtual element. Any boolean type other than 'abap_boolean' gives a deprecrated warning: 'Data Element xfeld is deprecated. Use Data Element ABAP_BOOLEAN instead', so we use abap_boolean. 

@EndUserText.label: 'Hide Section 1'
@UI.hidden: true
virtual hideSection1IsEnabled: abap_boolean,

 

In the metadata extension, we attempt to reference the virtual element but are given error 'Annotation value "hideSection1IsEnabled" must be of type 'Boolean'.@EndUserText.label: 'Hide Section 1' @ui.hidden: true virtual hideSection1IsEnabled: abap_boolean,  

  @UI.facet: [
  {
    parentId: 'Section1',
    id: 'SubSection1',
    type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,
    label: 'Sub Section 1',
    targetQualifier: 'DetailsGroup',
    hidden: 'hideSection1IsEnabled'
  },

__PRESENT

entry.title: Footer must be bottom of every page in reward statement
entry.content:
We have reward statement which contain of 5 pages. We want footer must be bottom of every page.

What will be the best code in XSL. I tried below, but for few employees working fine for few not.

<tr>
<td>
<p><br></br></p>
<p><br></br></p>
<p><br></br></p>
<p><br></br></p>
<p><br></br></p>
<p><br></br></p>
<p style="text-align: center; font-family: Ubuntu; font-size: 6.5pt; color: #2f5496;">Footer Address</p>
</td>
</tr>

@xavierlegarrec , need your help please.

entry.title: Re: Improving SAP Integration Suite with a sorting mechanism for DataStore entries
entry.content:
Excellent work, Marian! Just one remark: you write "Option 1 would always extract the last entry created". In SAP's documentation I read the following: 

There is no in-order processing of data store entries. In other words, the sequence of data store entries selected with a Select operation is random and non-deterministic. If you have restricted the number of entries to be fetched (with the Number of Polled Messages attribute), the selection of retrieved entries is also random. (https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/define-data-store-select-operations)

Wouldn't that mean that in most times that you do a select, you should theoretically get a different  message than before (random)? That means, I believe there shouldn't be the risk of a blocking situation (I haven't tested it and don't know how it behaves in reality).

However, of course if you need in sequence processing or have more control in general, your approach is perfect. 

Philippe

entry.title: Re: Generate IDOC from SAP BTP CAP app
entry.content:
Please call this 'MASTER_IDOC_DISTRIBUTE' from BTP:-

DATA: control TYPE edidc,
      data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF edidd.

control-mestyp = 'ORDERS'.
control-rcvprn = 'RECEIVER'.
control-sndprn = 'SENDER'.

APPEND VALUE #( segnam = 'E1EDK01' sdata = '...' ) TO data.

CALL FUNCTION 'MASTER_IDOC_DISTRIBUTE'
  EXPORTING
    master_idoc_control = control
  TABLES
    communication_idoc_control = control
    idoc_data_records = data.

Thanks

entry.title: How to migrate SAP BPC 10.1 to SAC planning & analytic platform
entry.content:
How to migrate SAP BPC 10.1 to SAC planning & analytic platform.

Kindly share the steps in details pls. 

Thank you

Sandy

entry.title: ABAP Dump LIST_TOO_MANY_LPROS:Modify Screen.
entry.content:
Dear ALl,

I am getting dump ABAP Dump LIST_TOO_MANY_LPROS at line MODIFY SCREEN.. 

Code as below:

      LOOP AT SCREEN.
        IF screen-name = '%#AUTOTEXT005'.  "Sdeshpan
          screen-invisible = ' '.  "Visisble NXT
          screen-active = '1'.
          MODIFY SCREEN.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.

Kindly help,

Regards.

entry.title: Re: How to Add Custom Button in BP Application ToolBar
entry.content: Hi Robert-Peter, I have a customer requirement to add a custom button in the BP Application Toolbar, similar to the 'General Data' and 'Company Code' buttons.

entry.title: In-House Cash - Refunds Process
entry.content:
Dear all,

Kindly ask your advice on how Refunds Process can be implemented in the In-House Cash?

The problem: Refunds (returns for payments which haven't reached Vendor) are posted into main IHC account with the Bank Statement. Now, they (ideally) need to be re-posted back to original Subsidiary Company and original Vendor as an open item. Which is now not happening.

Is there any way to setup this with the standard functionality? Which standard process suggest SAP for this case? Only manual process?

Thanks for your advices in advance.

Filip

entry.title: Re: No direct postings possible to G/L account XXXXX we a...
entry.content:
"Unfortunately the system did not record movement 202 in ERP, because a queue appeared. "

How did you handle this (before trying to create manual corrections)

  • SMQ1 outbound or SMQ2 inbound queue fails with SYSFAIL (EWM)

entry.title: Re: WESLOG XML not picking Material
entry.content:
Solved using method ANALYZE_CHGOBJ_MAT_FULL of class CL_WES_SERV_OUT_MATERIAL.

Added new added DMARC in when condition .

entry.title: Re: Handling File Routing and Alerts in SAP CPI Based on File Type
entry.content:
@harsh__chauhanit is now clear your requirement. Nevertheless, for safety and if it's the file type the relevant criteria, and since anyone can assign any file extention to any file type, then you may consider the use of the Apache Tika .

Apache Tika is a java library that you can use in a Groovy script and will help you to identify file types in a more secure way than by just looking at the file extension. Of course this may not be necessary if you trust the creators of the source files in the SFTP server.

entry.title: Catch weight quantity wrongly getting determined in outbound
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have one issue in which the material the Catch weight material but whenever I'm creating and outbound from STO the parallel unit of measure (CW) is getting calculated from material master 1CW = 480 KG but the actual CW quantity in MMBE is 420 KG (Actual HU Qty), previously it was correctly getting picked as per the quantity in MMBE. But now something happened it's calculating from master data and due to this the queues are getting stuck with error "valuation quantity becomes negative" due to less catch weight at S4.

Please suggest what need to be done in this case.

Thanks,

Vinod.

entry.title: Re: Passing Input parameters from analytic dataset to analytic model in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Thanks, it is of great help. I have done step by step as shown in this blog, the input parameter works fine and I can see the prompt in SAC. The issue is I do not see the Value Help, list of values in SAC. What should I be missing.
Regards
Arvind

entry.title: Re: How to consume Payroll data in CDS view ?
entry.content: Same requirement here too. We need the data from 0HR_PY_1_CE in Datasphere but it's not compatible (delta processing is not possible - would need BW bridge I think, which we don't have). Has anyone had any success building a CDS as an alternative?

entry.title: Re: Hide UI.Facet dynamically w/ ABAP CDS annotcation
entry.content:
Hi,

ADT expects a literal value(true/false), not reference.

To show/hide UI facets dynamically, I think custom logic at UI5 can be done.

Thanks,

Arun

entry.title: Re: BAS in BTP Trial Account is NOT getting STARTED
entry.content: The issue was resolved on its own after a day. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Functional Area in Asset Accounting - Message no. AAPO189
entry.content: any answer for this query

entry.title: Processing exception during a "Render" operation. .ProcessingException: IO error detected while open
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

 I am working on adobe form and I am facing system error  as show below.

 


 I have done all the three tests of ADS
FP_TEST_00
FP_CHECK_DESTINATION_SERVICE
FP_PDF_TEST_00

All the three reports are giving the results as expected .

Earlier I did not understand what the system error is about. After going through few threads I could able to trace from where the error is coming and what the error is about.

CREATE_OUTPUT_CURRENT_FORM is the subroutine where I could able to capture the error.

The following is the error :

Processing exception during a "Render" operation. Request start time: Tue Apr 08 14:40:29 IST 2025 com.adobe.ProcessingException: com.adobe.ProcessingException: IO error detected while opening DEST connection: http://100.64.109.143:8080/sap/bc/fp/form/layout/ZMATERIAL_EXP_QA_CHECK2.XDP?fp-language=EN&fp-mlayout-sup=X&fp-cacheinfo=2025-04-07T05:34:18Z$5ef403a9-256d-4ee3-aaef-7f7832056b50$0$en$v1.0$ Exception Stack Trace: com.adobe.

NOTE : I am getting this error only in quality system . It is working fine in Development system.
Can you please let me know what is the cause of this system error and how to fix it. ?


I could able to find one post where the issue is the same as above and solution was offered to open backend system port connection from portal side. The thread is the below one. It is not clear to how to do it.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/problem-trying-read-data-from-destination/qaq-p/4290340

entry.title: Re: Trip Data Inconsistencies
entry.content:
Hi,

 

According to your screenshot of TE cluster the trip has not been posted so far (RUNID is initial).

Thus just delete the trip and create it again.

 

Regards,

Monika

entry.title: Re: Issue with GA SAP S/4HANA - Users Not Visible
entry.content: Danke für deine Antwort. Ich habe verschiedene Benutzertypen eingetragen (Dialog A, System B, Kommunikation C, Service S und Referenz L). Das Feld Q_DATE habe ich angepasst, aber ich komme immer wieder zum gleichen Ergebnis.

entry.title: Re: Required SAP Notes for Transition to New EBS Formats
entry.content: Unfortunately you cannot find informations about "From dtazv to pain 001.001.09" (AXZ oder type GBICS4) for R/3+S/4. B1 isn't helpful.

entry.title: CORS and Access-Control-Allow-Origin in SAP Build APPS when I try to call SAP ABAP cloud odata
entry.content:
Dear Community 

I'm working on implementation of SAP Build Apps application low code based on ABAP on cloud odata service . 

I have created the destination in SAP BTP destination as follows : 

And I have tried to make a get call over this destination as follows : 

Unfortunatly I'm blocked and it does not work : 

I tried many workaround and it does not work . 

 

And samething when I used basic authentification it does not work . 

 

your urgent help is highly appreciated

entry.title: Re: Hide UI.Facet dynamically w/ ABAP CDS annotcation
entry.content:
@ui.facet: [{
    parentId: 'Section1',
    id: 'SubSection1',
    type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,
    label: 'Sub Section 1',
    targetQualifier: 'DetailsGroup',
    //hidden: 'hideSection1IsEnabled'
}]

extend via manifest.json(say a details page):-

"sap.ui.controllerExtensions": {
  "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage.view.Details": {
    "controllerName": "<controller_name>"
  }
}

in the actual controller after rendering:-

onAfterRendering: function () {
        var oView = this.getView();
        var oModel = oView.getModel(); // Assuming you use default model
        var oContext = oView.getBindingContext();

        // Read the field `hideSection1IsEnabled`
        var bHideSection = oModel.getProperty("hideSection1IsEnabled", oContext);

        // Get the SmartForm or Section control (adjust ID)
        var oSection = oView.byId("SubSection1"); // Use actual section ID

        if (oSection) {
          oSection.setVisible(!bHideSection); // Hide or show based on logic
        }
      }

entry.title: Re: Error creating Solution quotation as per SAP excercies in SAP leaning sytem-Incosistent data

Hi @YPBTP

Margit

entry.title: Re: Delivery doesn't pick from Q-Stock as intended for NB...
entry.content: In the SAP note you mentioned, it is explained to implement 3 other SAP notes in case you want to post from Quality stock.

entry.title: Re: Access value of badi parameter 'Type Ref to'
entry.content: Hi Dominik, I like to ask one more question please. If parameter "RE_POS_MAN_PROC" would be of type "Exporting". How to address it than? Thanks

entry.title: How to schedule fqm_delete for snapshot cleanup in public cloud system
entry.content:
1. App Delete data from one exposure from operations message log display "Execution is cancelled since regular deletion job is scheduled."
  2. FQM_DELETE log message is displayed on deletion of snapshot in public cloud "Execution is cancelled since regular deletion job is scheduled."
  3. The option to delete redundant snapshot is not available.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to schedule fqm_delete for snapshot cleanup in pu...
entry.content:
The FQM_DELETE program is scheduled as a background job variant SAP&REGU_14 and does not require manual execution for snapshot cleanup. It runs daily in the background, with snapshots configured to be deleted automatically based on the parameter "Number of days back to present = 14 days."

To change the scheduled start time, a request must be submitted via Expert Configuration.

Please find the information given in SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3593212

entry.title: Re: Internal external candidate
entry.content:
Hi Nimesha,

You can create a custom field in the candidate profile to capture this information. Using Integration Center along with Intelligent Services, you can trigger an integration that automatically updates this custom flag on the candidate profile whenever the candidate status changes.

 

Thanks,
Sanjeet

entry.title: Re: Please provide configurable material - which main com...
entry.content:
Hello,

please review the SAP Help about sales BOM functionality in S/4Hana Public Cloud: Bills of Material in Sales Documents 

You can achieve this by using item category group DISH:

Best regards

entry.title: Re: SAP Platinum Engineering Libraries
entry.content:
Hello,

Thanks, Melvin for this blog! I have some open points about the Email sending function.

I use the platinum library to send email but those are with the following limitations

  • Subject with max 50 characters
  • Attachment name max 50 characters
  • At the bottom of the mail, I will always have the text "This mail was sent frommyXXXXXX.businessbydesign.cloud.sap"

May be someone can help me finding any solution on how to extend the number of characters and also how to remove the text.

Or are there any other ways (libraries) to send email from pdi? Without using an external Service?

Many Thanks

Linda

entry.title: Re: Hide UI.Facet dynamically w/ ABAP CDS annotcation
entry.content: How would you toggle the UI.Hidden filter on/off if you can only set it with a literal value? Feels like a bug to not be able to pass a reference variable to me.

entry.title: Re: Renewal of CN=*.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap
entry.content: @abiskop, many thanks

entry.title: Re: CORS and Access-Control-Allow-Origin in SAP Build APP...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please use the relative url like '/destination/EMDEMO/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZSRV_EM_ORDERS_BIND' instead of the 'https://<backend>.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZSRV_EM_ORDERS_BIND'

CORS blocking due to direct calling of the url.

Thanks,

Arun

entry.title: Version to be selected
entry.content:
Customer goal is the standard subcontracting for intercompany process, especially from logistics point of view when the EWM storage location is used.

Customer have drawn the main steps of the intercompany subcontracting process from the business point of view.

Their main issues on “how to reflect these in SAP” are mention in the attached file.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Version to be selected
entry.content: Told the  customer to utilize the BADI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST method PROCESS_ACCOUNT for this purpose.  But they want the clarification.

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content: Hi experts, I need your help please.

entry.title: PP/DS: Forward scheduling doesn’t schedule as early as expected
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I’m testing the forward scheduling function in the PP/DS planning board when adding planned orders manually.

Even though the calendar is empty and forward scheduling is selected,
the system doesn’t place the order at the earliest possible start,
but somewhere later.

I left the start date empty and entered a requirement date,
but it still doesn’t behave the way I expected.

Does anyone know why this might happen,
or if there’s a good explanation or documentation for how these drag-and-drop scheduling options are supposed to work especially for forward and backward scheduling.

Thanks a lot!
